key_insight: A hybrid UNet-Transformer can translate composite microstructure images into stress and strain field maps with near-FEM accuracy for most tested geometries and loading conditions.
engineering_relevance: This enables fast surrogate-based screening and physics-informed design of composite materials by replacing expensive FEM evaluations with image-to-field prediction.
method: UNet encoder-decoder captures multiscale geometric structure
Transformer layers model long-range mechanical dependencies across the microstructure
Attention maps (Grad-CAM/Grad-CAM++) highlight phase boundaries and contact regions
evidence_signal: The model reported very strong results across most subdatasets, including R2 up to 0.9991, SSIM up to 0.9936, and MAE as low as 0.0050, with six of eight subdatasets below 0.05 normalized MAE.
watch_item: Performance drops on irregular square-grid cases with sparse soft-phase inclusions, indicating limited fidelity for sharp stress discontinuities and challenging geometries.
paper_title: Hybrid Unet-Transformer Model for Generating Stress and Strain Fields from Composite Geometrics
